prompt_text: Open source or affordable finance tools instead of QuickBooks
response:
# Budget-Friendly Finance Alternatives to QuickBooks

## Free Open-Source Options

**GnuCash**
- Desktop accounting software (Windows, Mac, Linux)
- Good for small businesses and personal finance
- Steep learning curve but very capable
- No cloud sync (desktop-only)

**Invoice Ninja**
- Open-source invoicing & time tracking
- Cloud-based with self-hosting option
- Free tier available
- Great for freelancers/service businesses

**Akaunting**
- Free, open-source invoicing platform
- Lightweight and simple
- Self-hosted or cloud
- Good starting point for beginners

## Affordable Cloud Options

**Wave**
- **Free** for invoicing, accounting, receipts
- Paid payroll add-on
- Great for very small businesses
- Simple, clean interface

**Zoho Books** (entry tier)
- ~$10-40/month depending on features
- Full accounting suite
- Integrates with other Zoho apps
- Good value for features

**Xero** (starter plan)
- ~$13-60/month
- Professional accounting software
- Better for growing businesses
- Strong integrations

## Spreadsheet-Based

**Google Sheets / Excel templates**
- Nearly free ($0-20/month)
- Full control and customization
- Manual data entry required
- Best for very simple needs

## Quick Comparison Table

| Tool | Cost | Best For | Complexity |
|------|------|----------|-----------|
| Wave | Free | Startups | Low |
| GnuCash | Free | Desktop users | High |
| Zoho | $10-40 | Growing small biz | Medium |
| Invoice Ninja | Free/paid | Freelancers | Medium |

**Which to choose?** Start with **Wave** if you want simplicity, or **GnuCash** if you prefer owning your data locally.
